helpers.name: get_items_for_task
helpers.purpose: Get items for task, optionally filtered by status

used_by_directives.description: Check for duplicate item names before creating new item - prevents naming conflicts
Check for duplicate item names before creating new item for subtask - prevents naming conflicts
Check for duplicate item names before creating new item for sidequest - prevents naming conflicts

helpers.name: get_incomplete_items
helpers.purpose: Get incomplete items for any parent type

used_by_directives.description: Mark all incomplete items as completed when task completes - batch completes remaining items
Mark all incomplete items as completed when subtask completes - batch completes remaining items
Mark all incomplete items as completed when sidequest completes - batch completes remaining items
Query incomplete items for status report - shows granular work-in-progress

helpers.name: add_item

parameters.name: reference_id
parameters.description: ID of the parent task, subtask, or sidequest

helpers.purpose: Create a single work item under a task, subtask, or sidequest. Items are the smallest trackable units of work.
helpers.error_handling: Return error if parent record not found or parent status is 'completed'
return_statements: Item created. Begin working on items: reserve_file → search_modules for overlap → write FP code → finalize_file → reserve/finalize functions+types → add_file_to_flow → add_file_to_module (if domain logic) → add_interactions → add_types_functions.
Item done → update_item(id=<item_id>, status='completed'). All items done → check get_incomplete_items(for_table, for_id) before completing parent.

used_by_directives.description: Create item record after validating parent exists and checking for duplicates
Create items for tasks during decomposition if item-level granularity is identified

implementation_notes: Returns {success: true, id: new_id}
SQL: INSERT INTO items (reference_table, reference_id, name, description, status) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?) RETURNING id
Validate reference_table is one of 'tasks', 'subtasks', 'sidequests'
Validate parent record exists and is not completed before inserting

helpers.name: add_items

parameters.name: reference_id
parameters.description: ID of the parent task, subtask, or sidequest

parameters.name: items
parameters.description: Array of {name: string, description?: string} objects

helpers.purpose: Batch-create multiple work items under a single parent in one call. All items created with status 'pending'.
helpers.error_handling: Return error if parent record not found or parent status is 'completed'. Atomic — if any item fails validation, none are inserted.
return_statements: Items created. Begin working on items: reserve_file → search_modules for overlap → write FP code → finalize_file → reserve/finalize functions+types → add_file_to_flow → add_file_to_module (if domain logic) → add_interactions → add_types_functions.
Item done → update_item(id=<item_id>, status='completed'). All items done → check get_incomplete_items(for_table, for_id) before completing parent.

used_by_directives.description: Batch-create items when multiple items identified during decomposition
Batch-create items for tasks during decomposition when multiple items are identified at once

implementation_notes: Returns {success: true, ids: [new_id_1, new_id_2, ...], count: N}
SQL: INSERT INTO items (reference_table, reference_id, name, description, status) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'pending') for each item in array
Validate reference_table is one of 'tasks', 'subtasks', 'sidequests'
Validate parent record exists and is not completed before inserting
Use transaction — all items inserted atomically (all succeed or all fail)
Validate each item has a non-empty 'name' field

helpers.name: update_item
helpers.purpose: Update a single item's status, name, or description. Primary use: marking items in_progress or completed as work progresses.
helpers.error_handling: Return error if item ID not found
return_statements: If status='completed': check get_incomplete_items(for_table=<reference_table>, for_id=<reference_id>) — if empty, all items done → complete the parent via update_task/update_subtask/update_sidequest(status='completed').
If status='in_progress': proceed with coding work for this item.

used_by_directives.description: Update individual item status during task completion workflow
Update individual item status during subtask completion workflow
Update individual item status during sidequest completion workflow

implementation_notes: Returns {success: true, id: id}
SQL: UPDATE items SET <fields> WHERE id=?
Only updates fields that are non-null — partial updates supported
Sets updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on every update

helpers.name: update_items

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Array of item IDs to update

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Field-value pairs to apply to all items (e.g., {status: 'completed'})

helpers.purpose: Batch-update multiple items with the same field values. Primary use: marking multiple items completed at once when parent completes.
helpers.error_handling: Return error if any ID not found. Atomic — all updates succeed or none apply.
return_statements: If status='completed' for all: check get_incomplete_items(for_table=<reference_table>, for_id=<reference_id>) — if empty, all items done → complete the parent via update_task/update_subtask/update_sidequest(status='completed').

used_by_directives.description: Batch-complete remaining items when task is marked completed
Batch-complete remaining items when subtask is marked completed
Batch-complete remaining items when sidequest is marked completed

implementation_notes: Returns {success: true, updated_count: N}
SQL: UPDATE items SET <fields> WHERE id IN (?, ?, ...) within a transaction
Only updates fields present in data object — partial updates supported
Validate all IDs exist before applying updates
Sets updated_at = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on every update
Use transaction — all updates applied atomically

helpers.name: delete_item
helpers.error_handling: Cannot delete in-progress/completed items, only pending

parameters.name: reference_id

parameters.name: send_with_directive

helpers.purpose: Record a decision, architecture change, or context event in the project's audit trail. Supports typed notes with optional directive linking.
helpers.error_handling: Returns error if project.db not initialized, otherwise continues silently

parameters_mapping.content: clarification, reasoning, or context to log

used_by_directives.description: Log clarifications, reasoning, and directive execution context to project.db notes table. Used by other directives for transparent reasoning trails.
Log sidequest outcome and lessons learned to notes table - captures exploratory work results for future reference

Search for specific metrics by keyword - targeted metric retrieval from notes

parameters.name: send_with_directive

helpers.name: create_state_database

parameters.name: source_directory
parameters.description: Absolute path to project source directory (from infrastructure.source_directory)

helpers.purpose: Create per-project state database (.state/runtime.db) from SQL schema. FP-compliant replacement for mutable global variables.
helpers.error_handling: Returns Result(success=False, error=...) if schema file missing or creation fails. Idempotent — returns success if database already exists.
return_statements: IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED: AI must now create the state_operations helper file in the project's .state/ directory BEFORE doing anything else. Do NOT defer this step, add it as a note, or move on to other work. Call get_state_operations_template() to retrieve the Python reference template, then create a language-appropriate version at <source-dir>/.state/state_operations.<ext>. The template content is only available via that helper — do not attempt to locate it in MCP server files.

After creating the state_operations file, track with AIMFP: register both .state/runtime.db and .state/state_operations.<ext> in project.db via the standard reserve → finalize flow (files and functions). This notifies watchdog of the new .state/ files so it does not emit warnings about untracked files.

used_by_directives.description: Create state database after source_directory is confirmed during infrastructure mapping

implementation_notes: Creates <source-dir>/.state/ directory and runtime.db using SQL schema at database/initialization/state_db.sql
Returns Result with data={created: bool, db_path: str, state_dir: str}
After creation, AI must IMMEDIATELY call get_state_operations_template() and create the language-specific state_operations file — do not defer
Schema includes: variables table (key-value with type), update trigger, schema_version table

After state DB and state_operations files are created, AI should register them in project.db files table via the standard reserve → finalize flow. The .state/ directory and its contents are project files that should be tracked.

helpers.name: get_state_operations_template
helpers.purpose: Return the Python state_operations.py template content for AI to adapt to the project's language. Use this to retrieve the CRUD helper template for .state/runtime.db.
helpers.error_handling: Returns Result(success=False, error=...) if template file not found.
return_statements: Template returned. AI must now create a language-appropriate version of this file at <source-dir>/.state/state_operations.<ext>. Preserve all five CRUD functions (set_var, get_var, delete_var, increment_var, list_vars) and the Result type. Adapt imports, types, and SQLite library to the project's primary language.
After writing the file, track with AIMFP: register it in project.db via the standard reserve → finalize flow (file and functions).

used_by_directives.description: Retrieve state operations template immediately after create_state_database, to create language-appropriate state helpers

implementation_notes: Reads src/aimfp/templates/state_db/state_operations.py and returns full content
Returns Result with data={template_content: str, functions: list, target_path: str}
No parameters — template is bundled with the AIMFP package
This helper exists so AI can retrieve the template at any point, not just from create_state_database return data

metadata.description: Item CRUD, note management operations, state database creation and template retrieval
metadata.notes: Added get_state_operations_template helper; removed dead initialize_state_database from metadata.py
